$comment: DISCLAIMER: This reference data is for informational purposes only. It does NOT constitute legal or tax advice. Tax laws change frequently — always verify with current legislation and qualified professionals. Data current as of March 2026.
title: Tax & Probate Form Requirements
description: Required tax returns, probate applications, and associated forms by jurisdiction, including thresholds that determine when each form is required.

GB.name: United Kingdom

forms.label: Inheritance Tax Account
forms.purpose: Full IHT return required when the estate does not qualify as an excepted estate, or when IHT is payable.
requiredWhen: Estate exceeds the nil rate band (£325,000) and does not qualify as excepted
IHT is payable
Certain reliefs (BPR, APR) are being claimed on amounts that take the gross estate above the excepted estate limits
Estate includes foreign assets exceeding £100,000
Specified transfers (gifts with reservation, etc.) exceed £250,000
forms.deadline: 12 months from the end of the month of death (but tax is due 6 months from the end of the month of death)

supplementaryForms.purpose: Claim to transfer unused nil rate band
Gifts and other transfers of value
Houses, land, buildings, and interests in land
Household and personal goods donated to charity
Unlisted stocks, shares, and control holdings
Business and partnership interests and assets

forms.label: Return of Estate Information (Excepted Estates — deaths before 1 January 2022)
forms.purpose: Simplified return for excepted estates where no IHT is payable. Replaced by the new excepted estate process for deaths on or after 1 January 2022.
forms.replacedBy: Online excepted estate reporting via the probate application (from 1 January 2022, most excepted estates no longer need a separate IHT form)
requiredWhen: Death occurred before 1 January 2022
Estate qualifies as excepted (gross value under IHT threshold or exempt/excepted by regulations)

forms.label: Claim to Transfer Unused Nil Rate Band (Excepted Estates)
forms.purpose: Used alongside IHT205 to claim the transfer of unused nil rate band from a predeceased spouse/civil partner, for excepted estates.
forms.replacedBy: Integrated into probate application process for deaths on or after 1 January 2022

forms.label: Probate Application (with a will — England & Wales)
forms.purpose: Application for a Grant of Probate where the deceased left a valid will. Now typically submitted online via the HMCTS probate service.
requiredWhen: Deceased left a valid will
Estate includes assets requiring a Grant of Probate to release (property, bank accounts above small estate thresholds, etc.)

forms.label: Probate Application (without a will — England & Wales)
forms.purpose: Application for a Grant of Letters of Administration where the deceased died intestate (without a valid will).
requiredWhen: Deceased died without a valid will (intestate)
Estate requires a Grant of Letters of Administration

forms.label: Confirmation Application (Scotland)
forms.purpose: Application for Confirmation (Scottish equivalent of probate). Includes inventory of the estate.

forms.label: Probate Application (Northern Ireland)
forms.purpose: Application for Grant of Probate or Letters of Administration in Northern Ireland.
forms.filedWith: Probate Office, Royal Courts of Justice, Belfast

GB.source: Inheritance Tax Act 1984; The Non-Contentious Probate Rules 1987; HMRC guidance IHT400 notes
GB.regulatoryBody: HM Revenue & Customs; HM Courts & Tribunals Service

US.name: United States

forms.label: United States Estate (and Generation-Skipping Transfer) Tax Return
forms.purpose: Federal estate tax return. Required for US citizens and residents whose gross estate plus adjusted taxable gifts exceeds the basic exclusion amount.
requiredWhen: Gross estate plus adjusted taxable gifts exceeds $13,610,000 (2024)
Even if no tax is due — required to elect portability of the unused exclusion to the surviving spouse
forms.deadline: 9 months from date of death (6-month extension available via Form 4768)

supplementaryForms.purpose: Estate tax return for non-resident aliens (estates exceeding $60,000)
Application for extension of time to file Form 706 and/or pay estate tax
Qualified Domestic Trust (QDOT) estate tax return
Generation-skipping transfer tax return for distributions
Generation-skipping transfer tax return for terminations

forms.label: United States Gift (and Generation-Skipping Transfer) Tax Return
forms.purpose: Required for gifts exceeding the annual exclusion ($18,000 per recipient in 2024), gift-splitting elections, or gifts of future interests.
requiredWhen: Gifts to any one person exceed the annual exclusion
Gift-splitting election with spouse
Gifts of future interests (regardless of amount)
Gifts to a non-citizen spouse exceeding $185,000 (2024)
forms.deadline: 15 April of the year following the gift (extensions available)

forms.label: US Income Tax Return for Estates and Trusts
forms.purpose: Income tax return for the estate as a separate taxable entity during the period of administration.
requiredWhen: Estate has gross income of $600 or more during the tax year
Any beneficiary is a non-resident alien

forms.label: State-Level Probate Applications
forms.purpose: Each state has its own probate court system and filing requirements. Common filings include petition for probate, inventory and appraisement, and accounting.

examples.threshold: Estates over $50,000 in personal property or any real property
No minimum — but small estates under $75,000 may use affidavit procedure
Summary administration available for estates under $75,000 or if decedent died more than 2 years ago

forms.note: Many states offer simplified procedures for small estates (variously called small estate affidavits, summary administration, or transfer-on-death provisions).

forms.label: State Estate or Inheritance Tax Returns
forms.purpose: States with their own estate or inheritance taxes require separate state returns.

US.source: Internal Revenue Code; IRS Instructions for Form 706; state-specific probate codes and tax statutes
US.regulatoryBody: Internal Revenue Service (IRS); state probate courts and tax authorities

AU.name: Australia

forms.label: Application for Grant of Probate / Letters of Administration
forms.purpose: Application to the Supreme Court of the relevant state or territory for authority to administer the estate.
requiredWhen: Estate includes real property
Financial institutions require a grant before releasing funds (threshold varies by institution, typically $50,000–$100,000)
forms.note: Each state and territory has its own forms and procedures. Many states have moved to online applications.

examples.form: Online application via NSW Online Registry

forms.label: Deceased's Final Income Tax Return
forms.purpose: Income tax return for the period from 1 July to date of death. Filed by the executor/administrator.
forms.deadline: By the regular lodgement date for the income year in which death occurred

forms.label: Trust Tax Return for the Deceased Estate
forms.purpose: If the estate earns income during the administration period, a trust tax return must be filed.
requiredWhen: Estate earns income during administration (interest, rent, dividends, etc.)

AU.source: State and territory Supreme Court rules; Income Tax Assessment Act 1997 (Cth)
AU.regulatoryBody: State/territory Supreme Courts; Australian Taxation Office (ATO)

CA.name: Canada

forms.label: Final Income Tax Return (T1)
forms.purpose: Income tax return for the period from 1 January to date of death. Reports all income including deemed dispositions.
forms.deadline: Later of 6 months after death or 30 April of the following year

forms.label: T3 Trust Income Tax and Information Return
forms.purpose: Filed for the estate if it earns income during the period of administration.

forms.label: Application for Certificate of Appointment of Estate Trustee (Ontario) / Grant of Probate (other provinces)
forms.purpose: Court application for authority to administer the estate. Name and process varies by province.
requiredWhen: Estate includes real property (in most provinces)
Financial institutions require a grant (typically for accounts exceeding $25,000–$50,000)
forms.note: Probate fees (estate administration tax) vary by province. Ontario charges $5 per $1,000 on the first $50,000 and $15 per $1,000 thereafter. British Columbia uses a graduated scale. Quebec uses a notarized will process that may avoid probate.

examples.form: Grant of Probate application (Court of King's Bench)

examples.note: Notarial wills do not require probate. Holograph and witnessed wills require verification (homologation) by the Superior Court.

CA.source: Income Tax Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. 1 (5th Supp.)); provincial Estate Administration Acts and Rules of Court
CA.regulatoryBody: Canada Revenue Agency (CRA); provincial Superior Courts

FR.name: France

forms.label: Déclaration de succession (Succession Declaration)
forms.purpose: Mandatory declaration of the estate's assets and liabilities for succession tax purposes.
requiredWhen: Always required unless the estate passes entirely to the surviving spouse/PACS partner and the gross value is below €50,000
Threshold for other heirs: required when the gross value exceeds €3,000 (€10,000 for direct heirs with no prior gifts)
forms.deadline: 6 months from date of death (12 months if death occurred outside metropolitan France)
forms.filedWith: Service des impôts des particuliers (local tax office)

forms.purpose: Main succession declaration
Supplementary declaration
Declaration of real property

forms.label: Acte de notoriété (Certificate of Heirship)
forms.purpose: Notarial deed establishing who the heirs are and their respective shares. Required to access bank accounts and transfer property.
requiredWhen: Always required in practice for any estate of substance
Banks require it to release funds

FR.source: Code général des impôts, Articles 641, 800; Code civil, Articles 730-1 to 730-5

forms.label: Erbschaftsteuererklärung (Inheritance Tax Return)
forms.purpose: Inheritance tax return required when requested by the tax office (Finanzamt) or when the inherited amount exceeds the tax-free allowance.
requiredWhen: Inheritance exceeds the personal tax-free allowance
Requested by the Finanzamt
Business property relief is claimed
forms.deadline: 3 months from notification by the Finanzamt (Finanzamt sets the specific deadline)

forms.label: Erbschein (Certificate of Inheritance)
forms.purpose: Court certificate confirming the heir(s) and their shares. Required to access bank accounts and transfer property where no notarial will or contract of inheritance exists.
requiredWhen: No notarial will (öffentliches Testament) or contract of inheritance (Erbvertrag) exists
Banks or land registry require proof of heirship
forms.filedWith: Nachlassgericht (probate court — a division of the Amtsgericht)
forms.note: Where a notarial will exists, the probate court issues a Testamentseröffnungsprotokoll (will opening protocol) which banks typically accept in lieu of an Erbschein.

forms.label: Notification of Inheritance
forms.purpose: Every heir must notify the local tax office of the inheritance within 3 months. This is separate from the tax return.
forms.deadline: 3 months from knowledge of the inheritance

IN.name: India

forms.label: Succession Certificate (Indian Succession Act, s. 372)
forms.purpose: Court certificate establishing the right of the petitioner to inherit debts and securities of the deceased.
requiredWhen: Required to transfer financial assets (shares, bank deposits, securities) of the deceased
No valid will exists, or the will is not registered
forms.filedWith: District Court with jurisdiction over the deceased's domicile
forms.note: Not required for property passing under a registered will with a named executor.

forms.label: Legal Heir Certificate
forms.purpose: Certificate establishing legal heirs for purposes of claiming provident fund, insurance, pension, and other employment benefits.

forms.label: Probate of Will
forms.purpose: Court certification of the validity of a will and appointment of the executor. Mandatory in certain jurisdictions (Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai, and the former presidency towns) under the Indian Succession Act.
requiredWhen: Will of a Hindu, Buddhist, Sikh, or Jain in Mumbai, Kolkata, or Chennai (compulsory probate jurisdictions)
All wills by Indian Christians, Jews, and Parsis anywhere in India

forms.label: Income Tax Return of the Deceased
forms.purpose: Final income tax return for the period up to date of death, filed by the legal representative.

IN.source: Indian Succession Act 1925; Hindu Succession Act 1956; Income Tax Act 1961
IN.regulatoryBody: District and High Courts; Income Tax Department

JP.name: Japan

forms.label: 相続税申告書 (Inheritance Tax Return)
forms.purpose: Required when the total value of the estate exceeds the basic deduction (¥30,000,000 + ¥6,000,000 × number of statutory heirs).
requiredWhen: Estate value exceeds basic deduction
Claiming spouse tax credit or small-scale residential land reduction (must file even if no tax is due)
forms.deadline: 10 months from the date the heir became aware of the death
forms.filedWith: Tax office (zeimusho) with jurisdiction over the deceased's domicile

forms.label: 準確定申告 (Quasi-Final Income Tax Return)
forms.purpose: Income tax return for the deceased for the period from 1 January to date of death.
forms.deadline: 4 months from the date the heir became aware of the death

forms.label: 遺産分割協議書 (Estate Division Agreement)
forms.purpose: Written agreement among all statutory heirs on the division of the estate. Required for property registration and may be required by financial institutions.
requiredWhen: Estate is to be divided differently from statutory shares
Required for real property transfer registration
Banks require it to release funds
forms.note: All heirs must sign and affix their registered personal seal (jitsu-in). Each heir's certificate of seal registration (inkan shōmeisho) must be attached.

JP.source: Sōzokuzei-hō (相続税法); Shotokuzei-hō (所得税法); Minpō (民法), Articles 906–914
JP.regulatoryBody: National Tax Agency (国税庁); Legal Affairs Bureau (法務局)

SG.name: Singapore

forms.label: Grant of Probate / Letters of Administration
forms.purpose: Court order authorising the executor (probate) or administrator (letters of administration) to deal with the deceased's estate.
requiredWhen: Estate includes real property
Financial institutions require a grant (typically for accounts exceeding $50,000)
CPF monies exceeding $50,000 without a valid nomination
forms.note: For Muslim deceased persons, inheritance is administered under the Inheritance Certificate issued by the Syariah Court, in accordance with Muslim law.

forms.label: Schedule of Assets (Form 8A/8B)
forms.purpose: List of all assets and liabilities of the deceased, filed with the probate application.
forms.filedWith: Family Justice Courts (as part of probate application)

forms.label: Public Trustee Administration (Estates under $50,000)
forms.purpose: For estates valued at $50,000 or less, the Public Trustee can administer the estate without a court grant.
requiredWhen: Estate value does not exceed $50,000
forms.filedWith: Public Trustee's Office, Ministry of Law

SG.source: Probate and Administration Act 1934; Intestate Succession Act 1967; Administration of Muslim Law Act 1966
SG.regulatoryBody: Family Justice Courts; Public Trustee's Office; Syariah Court (for Muslim estates)

IE.name: Ireland

forms.label: Application for Grant of Probate / Letters of Administration
forms.purpose: Application to the Probate Office or District Probate Registry for authority to administer the estate.
requiredWhen: Estate includes real property
Financial institutions require a grant (typically for accounts over €25,000)
forms.filedWith: Probate Office (High Court) or District Probate Registry

forms.label: Capital Acquisitions Tax Return (Form IT38)
forms.purpose: Self-assessment return for CAT on gifts and inheritances. Required when the taxable value exceeds 80% of the relevant group threshold.
requiredWhen: Taxable value of gift/inheritance exceeds 80% of the relevant group threshold
Any CAT is payable
Claiming agricultural or business relief
forms.deadline: 31 October of the year following the valuation date (or extended deadline if filing via ROS — Revenue Online Service)

forms.label: Inland Revenue Affidavit (Form CA24)
forms.purpose: Sworn statement of the deceased's assets and liabilities, filed as part of the probate application. Used to assess estate duty / CAT.

forms.label: Income Tax Return of the Deceased (Form 11/12)
forms.purpose: Final income tax return for the period up to date of death.

IE.source: Succession Act 1965; Capital Acquisitions Tax Consolidation Act 2003; Taxes Consolidation Act 1997

NZ.name: New Zealand

forms.label: Application for Probate / Letters of Administration
forms.purpose: Application to the High Court for authority to administer the estate.
requiredWhen: Estate includes real property
Financial institutions require a grant (thresholds vary, typically NZ$15,000–$30,000)
forms.note: Online applications are available via the Courts of New Zealand website.

forms.label: Election to Administer (Summary Administration)
forms.purpose: Simplified procedure for small estates. Administrator files an election instead of applying for full probate.
requiredWhen: Gross estate does not exceed $15,000
forms.filedWith: Public Trust or filed with relevant asset holders

forms.label: Individual Income Tax Return (IR3) — Deceased
forms.purpose: Final income tax return for the deceased for the period up to date of death.

forms.label: Estate or Trust Income Tax Return (IR6)
forms.purpose: Income tax return for the estate if it earns income during the administration period.

NZ.source: Administration Act 1969; High Court Rules 2016; Income Tax Act 2007
NZ.regulatoryBody: High Court of New Zealand; Inland Revenue (Te Tari Taake)
